summary: Comprehensive testing completed for Telegram risk alerts configuration panel feature. All 20 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests passed - all Telegram panel elements present and functional with proper data-testid attributes.
passed_tests: GET /api/telegram/status - returns 200 with correct structure (configured, bot_token_set, chat_id_set, daily_alerts_sent)
POST /api/telegram/config - saves bot_token and chat_id, returns updated status
POST /api/telegram/config - persistence verified via status endpoint
POST /api/telegram/test - sends test message (fallback mode since no real token)
GET /api/telegram/history - alert items have correct structure
Frontend: data-testid='save-telegram-config-btn' clickable and saves config
Frontend: data-testid='kill-switch-btn' present and shows correct state
Core APIs: health, prices, signals, events, risk status all working
Dashboard loads without errors - header, price panels, signals, risk control all render
Chinese Mandarin UI displays correctly throughout
seed_data_creation: None required - Telegram config is stored in memory
context_for_next_testing_agent: Telegram panel feature fully tested. All 4 Telegram API endpoints working: GET /api/telegram/status, POST /api/telegram/config, POST /api/telegram/test, GET /api/telegram/history. Frontend TelegramPanel component renders with all required data-testid attributes. Note: Telegram messages fall back to logging since no real bot token is configured (MOCKED). Market data is also simulated.

mocked_apis_list: Market price data (simulated - no real Twelve Data API key)
Telegram messages (fallback to log - no real bot token configured)
